prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "直腿弹跳" (straight-leg bounding / pogo run) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person mid-air during straight-leg bounding: both legs nearly fully extended (only slight knee bend), the front (left) leg swinging forward with toes pulled up; the rear (right) leg behind with foot pointed; both arms in running-style opposition; this is a stiff-legged bounding drill emphasizing ankle stiffness and elastic recoil; torso upright with slight forward lean; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus) for elastic recoil and hamstring group. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and quadriceps. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "小腿三头肌" pointing to the calf muscles for elastic recoil; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group; "臀大肌 / 股四头肌" pointing to the glute and quadriceps area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
